title: Hypenia calycina
taxonomy: Hypenia calycina is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Hypenia, which is a part of the tribe Ocimeae. The classification of Hypenia calycina is further detailed within the subfamily Nepetoideae. Hypenia calycina is a member of the clade comprising the families Lamiaceae and Verbenaceae. The plant's classification is essential for understanding its evolutionary relationships and characteristics.

intro: Hypenia calycina, also known as Brazilian jasmine or pink hypenia, is a flowering plant species native to Brazil, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in the treatment of various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Hypenia calycina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Hypenia calycina include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote relaxation, soothe digestive issues, and boost the immune system.
